scope_rows.question: What type of app is being built?
scope_rows.answer: B2B school-facing Olympiad operations portal with internal staff workflows
scope_rows.build_instruction: Build separate school and staff journeys using shared Directus backend.
scope_rows.test_instruction: Verify routing separates school and staff areas.

scope_rows.question: What is the MVP outcome?
scope_rows.answer: One school can register, upload students, confirm count, pay, book an exam slot, download exam material, submit courier info, view results, and download certificates.
scope_rows.build_instruction: Use this as the end-to-end acceptance path for build orchestration.
scope_rows.test_instruction: Run full E2E acceptance test from school login to certificate download.

scope_rows.question: What is explicitly out of scope for MVP?
scope_rows.answer: Online proctoring, parent/student accounts, mobile app, automated split settlements, AI marking, courier API integration, complex ERP accounting.
scope_rows.build_instruction: Do not scaffold routes or collections for out-of-scope items unless placeholder-only.
scope_rows.test_instruction: Scan routes and schema for out-of-scope modules.

scope_rows.question: What is the first build milestone?
scope_rows.answer: Directus schema, roles, school-level permissions, and basic school portal login/dashboard.
scope_rows.build_instruction: Prioritise schema/security before screens beyond login/dashboard.
scope_rows.test_instruction: Run login and school isolation tests.

scope_rows.question: What is the second build milestone?
scope_rows.answer: Student upload, validation, final count, fee calculation, and exam-slot selection without payment integration.
scope_rows.build_instruction: Build school journey with mocked payment status after schema is stable.
scope_rows.test_instruction: Run CSV validation and exam slot capacity tests.

scope_rows.question: What is the third build milestone?
scope_rows.answer: Razorpay payment link, webhook handler, material gating, and audit logs.
scope_rows.build_instruction: Add payment integration only after internal workflow is testable.
scope_rows.test_instruction: Run webhook verification and gate tests.

scope_rows.question: What should remain manual in MVP?
scope_rows.answer: OMR scanning, OMR exception review, result approval, and certificate final approval remain staff-controlled.
scope_rows.build_instruction: Create upload/import workflows, not automated OMR engine integration in MVP.

out_of_scope: Online proctoring, parent/student accounts, mobile app, automated split settlements, AI marking, courier API integration, complex ERP accounting.

architecture.deployment_target: Hostinger VPS or equivalent Node-capable deployment

modularity.question: How should the specification be structured?
modularity.answer: Use versioned modular specs under /spec/core, /spec/modules, /spec/security, /spec/changes, /spec/tests, and /spec/runbooks.
modularity.build_instruction: Create folders and generate one manifest per module; do not use one giant spec file.

modularity.question: Should generated JSON/MD files be versioned?
modularity.answer: Yes, every module has module_id, version, status, owner, dependencies, provides, timestamps, and change policy.
modularity.build_instruction: Add metadata block to each generated module file.

modularity.question: Can the builder overwrite the full app blindly?
modularity.answer: No. It must read existing specs, generate impact analysis, apply approved changes only, and preserve backward-compatible modules.
modularity.build_instruction: Implement runbook with incremental tasks and approvals for destructive changes.
modularity.test_instruction: Test runbook refuses destructive overwrite without approval.

modularity.question: What versioning policy applies?
modularity.answer: Semantic versioning: major for breaking schema/workflow/API changes, minor for new compatible features, patch for bug fixes.
modularity.build_instruction: Store version policy in project manifest and release notes template.
modularity.test_instruction: Check generated changelog follows semver rules.

modularity.question: How should future features be added?
modularity.answer: As new module folders with module.json, schema.json, workflows.json, permissions.json, security.json, and tests.json.
modularity.build_instruction: Require feature_request.json before new module generation.
modularity.test_instruction: Test new module has required files.

modularity.question: How should bug fixes be recorded?
modularity.answer: Every bug fix requires bug_id, affected module, severity, expected behavior, actual behavior, root cause, fix list, security impact, status, and regression tests.
modularity.build_instruction: Create bug_fixes.json structure and regression test requirement.

security_baseline_refs.question: What is the default security policy?
security_baseline_refs.answer: Deny by default; public access disabled except explicit certificate verification route.
security_baseline_refs.build_instruction: Set Directus public role to no access except approved public verification fields later.
security_baseline_refs.test_instruction: Permission test public role cannot read private collections.

security_baseline_refs.question: Are API secrets allowed in browser code?
security_baseline_refs.answer: No. All vendor secrets and Directus admin tokens must remain server-side only.
security_baseline_refs.build_instruction: Use Next.js server routes/actions as API proxy; never expose service token to client bundle.
security_baseline_refs.test_instruction: Scan code for secret variable usage in client components.

security_baseline_refs.question: What audit principle applies?
security_baseline_refs.answer: Log login, failed login, role changes, permission changes, payment changes, result changes, certificate generation, and file downloads.
security_baseline_refs.build_instruction: Create audit_events collection and call audit helper for critical actions.
security_baseline_refs.test_instruction: Verify audit rows for critical workflow tests.

all_decisions.question: What is the project code?
all_decisions.answer: VERSA_OLYMPIADS
all_decisions.build_instruction: Use project code in generated config, file prefixes, and documentation.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Validate project code appears in generated manifest.

all_decisions.question: What is the product name?
all_decisions.answer: Versa Olympiads
all_decisions.build_instruction: Display product name in portal header, login page, metadata, and emails.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Check visible product name across core pages.

all_decisions.question: What is the operating brand?
all_decisions.answer: Finverse Academy / Versa Olympiads
all_decisions.build_instruction: Allow brand labels to be configured from environment or settings collection.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Check brand config renders consistently.

all_decisions.question: What is the business owner?
all_decisions.answer: Fintermatics Education LLP / Finverse Academy owner
all_decisions.build_instruction: Store business owner in project_manifest spec only, not in public UI unless needed.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Verify manifest contains owner metadata.

all_decisions.question: What type of app is being built?
all_decisions.answer: B2B school-facing Olympiad operations portal with internal staff workflows
all_decisions.build_instruction: Build separate school and staff journeys using shared Directus backend.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Verify routing separates school and staff areas.

all_decisions.question: What is the MVP outcome?
all_decisions.answer: One school can register, upload students, confirm count, pay, book an exam slot, download exam material, submit courier info, view results, and download certificates.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Use this as the end-to-end acceptance path for build orchestration.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Run full E2E acceptance test from school login to certificate download.

all_decisions.question: What is explicitly out of scope for MVP?
all_decisions.answer: Online proctoring, parent/student accounts, mobile app, automated split settlements, AI marking, courier API integration, complex ERP accounting.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Do not scaffold routes or collections for out-of-scope items unless placeholder-only.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Scan routes and schema for out-of-scope modules.

all_decisions.question: What backend platform is used?
all_decisions.answer: Directus Cloud as headless backend for schema, auth, roles, files, flows, and APIs.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Connect using Directus SDK/server-side API routes; never expose admin tokens in browser.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Verify API calls route through server-side code.

all_decisions.question: What frontend stack is used?
all_decisions.answer: Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Scaffold Next.js app with app router, typed API utilities, shadcn components, and responsive layouts.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Run build and route smoke tests.

all_decisions.question: What UI principle applies?
all_decisions.answer: Finverse owns branded UI/UX; Directus owns backend and staff data studio.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Build custom portal pages; use Directus Studio only for internal admin fallback.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Verify school users never see Directus Studio.

all_decisions.question: What deployment target is assumed?
all_decisions.answer: Hostinger VPS or equivalent Node-capable deployment for Next.js portal; Directus Cloud for backend.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Generate deployment notes and environment variables for Hostinger VPS.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Check .env.example contains required variables but no secrets.

all_decisions.question: What primary domain pattern is assumed?
all_decisions.answer: School portal: olympiad.finverseacademy.com; API proxy: same Next.js app; Directus: versaolympiads.directus.app.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Use environment variables for URLs; do not hardcode final custom domains except examples.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Check config is environment-driven.

all_decisions.question: What timezone is used?
all_decisions.answer: Asia/Kolkata for India school operations; store dates in UTC and display in configured local timezone.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Use date utilities and persist UTC timestamps.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Test date rendering around slot booking and deadlines.

all_decisions.question: What currency is used for MVP?
all_decisions.answer: INR.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Format payment values as INR and store minor units where payment provider requires.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Verify fee calculations and currency formatting.

all_decisions.question: What app language is used?
all_decisions.answer: English first; make labels configurable for future localisation.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Keep text in simple config or constants file for future localisation.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Check major labels come from reusable constants.

all_decisions.question: What build approach should Codex follow?
all_decisions.answer: Read CSV, generate JSON specs, create Directus schema via MCP, scaffold portal, test module by module, stop on unresolved failures.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Generate codex_runbook.md from these rows before coding.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Check runbook exists and contains stop conditions.

all_decisions.question: What data should not be collected in MVP?
all_decisions.answer: Student date of birth, unnecessary parent contact data, Aadhaar/government IDs, bank card data, medical data unless legally required.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Do not add fields for prohibited/unnecessary data.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Schema test confirms fields absent.

all_decisions.answer: OMR scanning, OMR exception review, result approval, and certificate final approval remain staff-controlled.
all_decisions.build_instruction: Create upload/import workflows, not automated OMR engine integration in MVP.
all_decisions.test_instruction: Verify no auto-publish after import.
